must_detect.matched_text: Control status: configuration drift ticket SEC‑2416 is closed and the SSO‑based export control is implemented; this is not an ongoing breach and no further customer notification is required under DPDPA.

Pte. (Singapore) triggered a cross-border transfer; the export ran via https://xprt.aravalli-orbit.example/xpt?req=DE-24-0615&ref=E U-TRN-7 3B though India-only routing was intended.
Dataset elements: names, corporate emails, masked PAN (P AN‑XXZ P9 1 0 3 Q), truncated Aadhaar (**** **** 9 9 8 1), and redacted GSTIN (G S T I N: XX‑XX‑XXXX‑XX), no bank a/c, no card, and no passwords.
A child applicant (self‑declared 17) applied to our campus ambassador onboarding; guardian consent recorded in the form and withdrawn on 01-Jun-2026 via ds ar portal, prompting immediate action.
Privacy workflow executed: DSAR ACK #DS-26-0611 sent 01-Jun-2026 10:12 IST; consent withdrawal CN-26-0612 honoured; minimisation applied to the batch and extraneous fields dropped.
Erasure completed for the child record and 2 adult applicants at 14:40 IST 01-Jun-2026; Erasure Cert E‑RS/24/0612 archived; retention tag DPA_S2_RET90 was not applied post‑erasure.
Cross-border transfer covered by DPA Schedule 2 (Cross‑Border Transfer) executed 19‑May‑2026 and Data Export Approval DE‑24‑0615; TI PIA‑IN‑24‑0518 shows residual risk “Low” and has been closed.
For avoidance of doubt, no UPSI under SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015 was part of the dataset; board note “Project Indigo capex revision” remained ring‑fenced in a separate repository and trading window closure procedures were already in force.
Security review indicates no credentials, no MAC addresses, and no device identifiers were collected; firewall logs show “src‑mac: 00‑00‑00‑00‑00‑00” placeholders only, which should not be treated as live data.
We also attached public‑source investor presentation (downloaded from the exchange website) and generic policy text; these are not personal data nor price‑sensitive.
Control status: configuration drift ticket SEC‑2416 is closed and the SSO‑based export control is implemented; this is not an ongoing breach and no further customer notification is required under DPDPA.
